questions.question: Differentiate between P2PKH and P2SH transaction types in Bitcoin. Explain the advantages of P2SH.
keyPoints: P2PKH: locks funds to a public key hash, recipient proves ownership with public key + signature.
P2SH: locks funds to a script hash, recipient provides the full redeem script when spending.
P2SH advantage: simpler addresses, supports complex conditions (multisig), shifts cost to spender.

questions.question: Describe the various types of blockchain transactions. Include at least four types with their purposes and use cases.
keyPoints: P2PKH: Standard payment from one address to another. Most common type.
Coinbase: First transaction in a block, creates new bitcoin as mining reward.
Multisig: Requires M-of-N signatures. Used for shared wallets, escrow, organizational security.
P2SH: Enables complex scripts. Used for multisig addresses, payment channels.
SegWit: Separates witness data. Fixes malleability, improves scalability.
Timelocked: Cannot be spent until a future time/block. Used in payment channels.
Ethereum contract transactions: Create contracts or call contract functions.

questions.question: What is a multisignature transaction? Explain its working with a 2-of-3 example and list two real-world use cases.
keyPoints: Requires M signatures out of N possible signers to authorize spending.
2-of-3 example: 3 keys created, any 2 can sign to spend (e.g., user + backup + exchange).
Use case 1: Corporate treasury requiring multiple board member approval.
Use case 2: Escrow service where buyer, seller, and arbitrator each hold one key.

questions.question: Compare Bitcoin and Ethereum transaction types. How does Ethereum extend transaction functionality beyond simple value transfers?
keyPoints: Bitcoin: primarily value transfers (P2PKH, P2SH, multisig) with limited scripting.
Ethereum: value transfers + contract creation + message calls to contracts.
Ethereum transactions include a data field for smart contract function calls.
Ethereum uses account model vs Bitcoin's UTXO model.
